case_laws.title: Pak Elektron Ltd v. SECP
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the powers of SECP to regulate listed companies. The court held that SECP has authority to investigate and penalize companies for non-compliance with disclosure requirements under the Companies Act 2017. Directors who fail to comply with statutory obligations can be personally liable.
case_laws.summary_ur: ایس ای سی پی کے لسٹڈ کمپنیوں کو ریگولیٹ کرنے کے اختیارات سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: SECP powers - Listed companies - Disclosure requirements - Directors' liability - Companies Act 2017
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Companies Act", "Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan Act"]

case_laws.title: Engro Corporation v. Minority Shareholders Association
case_laws.summary_en: Case on minority shareholder protection. The court held that minority shareholders have the right to challenge decisions made by the majority that are oppressive or prejudicial to their interests. Section 291 of the Companies Act 2017 provides remedies against oppressive conduct.
case_laws.summary_ur: اقلیتی شیئر ہولڈرز کے تحفظ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Minority shareholders - Oppressive conduct - Section 291 Companies Act - Prejudicial decisions - Remedies

case_laws.title: Faisalabad Industries v. Commissioner Income Tax
case_laws.summary_en: Case on corporate restructuring and tax implications. The court held that merger and amalgamation of companies under the Companies Act 2017 should not attract capital gains tax on the transfer of assets between merging entities, as the transaction is not a transfer in the commercial sense.
case_laws.summary_ur: کارپوریٹ تنظیم نو اور ٹیکس اثرات سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Merger - Amalgamation - Capital gains tax - Not a transfer - Companies Act 2017 - Section 282

case_laws.title: Competition Commission v. Cartel Industries Group
case_laws.summary_en: Case on anti-competitive practices under the Competition Act 2010. The court upheld CCP's order penalizing companies for price-fixing cartel. The court held that horizontal agreements between competitors to fix prices, limit production, or allocate markets are per se illegal.
case_laws.summary_ur: مسابقتی ایکٹ 2010 کے تحت غیر مسابقتی طرز عمل سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Anti-competitive practices - Price fixing - Cartel - Competition Act 2010 - Per se illegal - CCP powers

case_laws.title: Ali Partners v. Bashir Trading Co.
case_laws.summary_en: Case on dissolution of partnership and settlement of accounts under the Partnership Act 1932. The court held that upon dissolution, partnership assets must be applied first to pay firm's debts, then to pay each partner's advances, and remaining balance distributed according to profit-sharing ratio.
case_laws.summary_ur: شراکت داری کی تحلیل اور حسابات کے تصفیے سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Partnership dissolution - Settlement of accounts - Firm's debts first - Advances - Profit sharing ratio - Partnership Act 1932

case_laws.title: Digital Services Ltd v. SECP
case_laws.summary_en: Case on winding up of company and distribution of assets. The court discussed the order of priority for distribution of assets: secured creditors, cost of winding up, preferential payments, unsecured creditors, and finally shareholders.
case_laws.summary_ur: کمپنی کی ختمی اور اثاثوں کی تقسیم سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Winding up - Distribution of assets - Priority order - Secured creditors - Preferential payments - Companies Act 2017

case_laws.title: Fauji Foundation v. Workers Union
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the fiduciary duties of company directors. The court held that directors owe duties of care, skill, and diligence to the company. A director who acts in conflict of interest or derives personal benefit from company transactions is liable for breach of fiduciary duty.
case_laws.summary_ur: کمپنی کے ڈائریکٹرز کے امانتی فرائض سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Directors' fiduciary duties - Care skill diligence - Conflict of interest - Personal benefit - Breach liability

case_laws.title: Fatima Enterprises v. FBR
case_laws.summary_en: Case regarding the validity of tax assessment orders and taxpayer's rights to appeal. The court held that a tax assessment order must be based on proper inquiry and rational basis. Arbitrary tax demands violate the taxpayer's rights under Articles 4 and 25 of the Constitution.
case_laws.summary_ur: ٹیکس تشخیص کے احکامات کی قانونی حیثیت سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Tax assessment - FBR - Taxpayer rights - Constitutional protection - Income Tax Ordinance 2001 - Right to appeal

case_laws.title: Habib Bank Ltd v. Commissioner Inland Revenue
case_laws.summary_en: Case on withholding tax obligations of banks under the Income Tax Ordinance. The court held that banks are required to deduct tax at source on profit from deposits. Failure to deduct and deposit tax makes the bank liable for the amount of tax and penalties.
case_laws.summary_ur: انکم ٹیکس آرڈیننس کے تحت بینکوں کی ود ہولڈنگ ٹیکس ذمہ داریوں سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Withholding tax - Banks - Tax at source - Profit on deposits - Failure to deduct - Penalties

case_laws.title: Textile Mills Association v. Federal Board of Revenue
case_laws.summary_en: Case on sales tax refund claims. The court held that FBR cannot withhold legitimate refund claims of taxpayers beyond the statutory period. Delayed refunds carry markup at the prescribed rate. The court directed FBR to process pending refund claims within 60 days.
case_laws.summary_ur: سیلز ٹیکس ریفنڈ کلیمز سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Sales tax refund - FBR delay - Statutory period - Markup on delayed refunds - Direction to process within 60 days

case_laws.title: Chamber of Commerce v. Federal Government
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case on the vires of super tax imposed under Section 4-C of the Income Tax Ordinance. The court examined whether the super tax is confiscatory and violates Article 23 and 24 of the Constitution. The court held that while Parliament has wide taxing powers, taxes cannot be so excessive as to be confiscatory.
case_laws.summary_ur: انکم ٹیکس آرڈیننس کی دفعہ 4-سی کے تحت عائد سپر ٹیکس کی قانونی حیثیت سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Super tax - Confiscatory tax - Article 23, 24 Constitution - Parliamentary taxing powers - Reasonable limits

case_laws.title: Import Corporation v. Collector Customs
case_laws.summary_en: Case on customs valuation and classification disputes. The court held that the valuation of imported goods must be based on transaction value under the Customs Act. If customs authorities reject the declared value, they must provide cogent reasons and rely on alternative valuation methods prescribed by law.
case_laws.summary_ur: کسٹمز ویلیوایشن اور درجہ بندی کے تنازعات سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Customs valuation - Transaction value - Rejection of declared value - Alternative methods - Customs Act 1969

case_laws.title: Real Estate Developers v. Punjab Revenue Authority
case_laws.summary_en: Case on stamp duty payable on property conveyance. The court held that stamp duty must be paid on the true market value of the property, not on the understated value mentioned in the sale deed. Under-stamped documents are inadmissible in evidence.
case_laws.summary_ur: جائیداد کی فروخت پر قابل ادائیگی سٹیمپ ڈیوٹی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Stamp duty - Market value - Under-stamped documents - Inadmissibility - Stamp Act 1899

case_laws.title: Pharmaceutical Association v. FBR
case_laws.summary_en: Case on exemption from sales tax on essential medicines. The court upheld the exemption notification for life-saving drugs and held that FBR cannot withdraw tax exemptions on essential medicines without proper consultation and without considering the impact on public health.
case_laws.summary_ur: ضروری ادویات پر سیلز ٹیکس سے استثنیٰ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Sales tax exemption - Essential medicines - Life-saving drugs - FBR notification - Public health consideration

case_laws.title: Manufacturing Corp v. Additional Commissioner
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the powers of tax authorities to make best judgment assessment. The court held that best judgment assessment under Section 122(5) ITO is only permissible when the taxpayer fails to file return or comply with notices. The assessment must still be based on available information and cannot be arbitrary.
case_laws.summary_ur: ٹیکس حکام کے بہترین فیصلے کی بنیاد پر تشخیص کے اختیارات سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Best judgment assessment - Section 122(5) ITO - Failure to file - Based on available information - Not arbitrary

case_laws.title: Workers Federation v. Federation of Pakistan
case_laws.summary_en: Case regarding labor rights and the right to form trade unions under Article 17 of the Constitution. The Supreme Court upheld the fundamental right of workers to form associations and trade unions, holding that restrictions must be reasonable and proportionate.
case_laws.summary_ur: آئین کی دفعہ 17 کے تحت مزدور حقوق اور ٹریڈ یونین بنانے کے حق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Labor rights - Trade unions - Article 17 - Freedom of association - Reasonable restrictions

case_laws.title: Karachi Electric Workers Union v. Karachi Electric Supply Co.
case_laws.summary_en: Case on unfair labor practices by employer. The court held that termination of an employee for participating in lawful trade union activities constitutes unfair labor practice under Section 32 of the Industrial Relations Act 2012. The employee is entitled to reinstatement with back wages.
case_laws.summary_ur: آجر کے غیر منصفانہ مزدور طرز عمل سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Unfair labor practice - Termination for union activity - Section 32 IRA - Reinstatement - Back wages

case_laws.title: Pakistan International Airlines v. PIA Employees Union
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the legality of mass termination of employees. The court held that large-scale termination without following due process, including notice and opportunity to be heard, is illegal. The employer must comply with standing orders and relevant labor laws before terminating employees.
case_laws.summary_ur: بڑے پیمانے پر ملازمین کی برطرفی کی قانونی حیثیت سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Mass termination - Due process - Notice required - Standing orders - Labor law compliance

case_laws.title: Textile Workers Federation v. Pakistan Textile Mills
case_laws.summary_en: Case on minimum wage compliance. The court held that payment below the statutory minimum wage is illegal and the employer is liable to pay the difference along with compensation. Workers cannot waive their right to minimum wage as it is a statutory protection.
case_laws.summary_ur: کم از کم اجرت کی تعمیل سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Minimum wage - Statutory protection - Cannot be waived - Employer liability - Payment of difference - Compensation

case_laws.title: Factory Workers v. National Industries
case_laws.summary_en: Case on workplace safety and employer's liability for industrial accidents. The court held that the employer has a statutory duty to provide safe working conditions under the Factories Act 1934. Failure to maintain safety standards resulting in worker injury makes the employer liable for compensation under the Workmen's Compensation Act.
case_laws.summary_ur: کام کی جگہ کی حفاظت اور صنعتی حادثات میں آجر کی ذمہ داری سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Workplace safety - Factories Act - Employer's duty - Industrial accidents - Workmen's Compensation Act

case_laws.title: Government School Teachers v. Government of Punjab
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the right to pension and retirement benefits of government employees. The court held that pension is a constitutional right and a deferred portion of compensation. It cannot be withheld or reduced arbitrarily. Departmental proceedings after retirement must not delay pension payment.
case_laws.summary_ur: سرکاری ملازمین کی پنشن اور ریٹائرمنٹ مراعات کے حق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Pension - Constitutional right - Deferred compensation - Cannot be withheld - Departmental proceedings

case_laws.title: Contract Employees Association v. WAPDA
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the rights of contract employees and regularization. The court held that employees who have worked continuously for more than the prescribed period are entitled to regularization. Contract renewal beyond the statutory limit creates a presumption of permanent employment.
case_laws.summary_ur: ٹھیکہ ملازمین کے حقوق اور ریگولرائزیشن سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Contract employees - Regularization - Continuous service - Prescribed period - Presumption of permanent employment

case_laws.title: Hospital Nurses Association v. Punjab Healthcare Commission
case_laws.summary_en: Case on working hours and overtime compensation. The court held that working beyond 48 hours per week entitles the employee to overtime at double the ordinary rate of wages. The employer cannot compel workers to work overtime regularly without consent.
case_laws.summary_ur: کام کے اوقات اور اوور ٹائم معاوضے سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Working hours - 48 hours limit - Overtime double rate - Employer cannot compel - Factories Act Section 47
